# rex

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 248
- **Lines of code**: 84,274
- **Languages**: TypeScript (231), JSON (8), Other (4), Markdown (4), Text (1)
- **Zones**: 15 (15 with descriptions)
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-10T03:05:55.767Z

## Architecture Zones

### CLI and MCP Interface (`cli-mcp-interface`)

Command-line entry points and MCP tool definitions for rex operations including PRD reshaping, pruning, health checks, chunked review, smart-add, and the MCP server interface.

- Files: 23
- Cohesion: 0.27, Coupling: 0.73
- Entry points: src/analyze/reshape-reason.ts, src/cli/commands/chunked-review.ts, src/cli/commands/constants.ts, src/cli/commands/health.ts, src/cli/commands/init.ts (+7 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/cli-mcp-interface/context.md`

### Integration Adapter Layer (`integration-adapter-layer`)

Manages remote sync adapters, integration schemas, and the adapter registry for connecting the PRD to external tools like Jira and Notion.

- Files: 16
- Cohesion: 0.38, Coupling: 0.63
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/sync.ts, src/store/adapter-registry.ts, src/store/index.ts, src/store/integration-schema.ts, src/store/integration-schemas/index.ts (+3 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/integration-adapter-layer/context.md`

### LLM Analysis Engine (`llm-analysis-engine`)

Orchestrates LLM-driven PRD analysis: bridges raw scan output to the LLM, validates and reasons about proposed modifications, and guards against unsafe consolidations.

- Files: 28
- Cohesion: 0.38, Coupling: 0.62
- Entry points: src/analyze/analyze-shared.ts, src/analyze/consolidation-guard.ts, src/analyze/file-validation.ts, src/analyze/index.ts, src/analyze/llm-bridge.ts (+7 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/llm-analysis-engine/context.md`

### Notion Integration Client (`notion-integration-client`)

Encapsulates the low-level Notion API client and its associated store-contract tests, forming the isolated foundation layer of the Notion adapter.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Entry points: src/store/notion-client.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/notion-integration-client/context.md`

### PRD Domain Operations (`prd-domain-operations`)

The central domain layer for rex: schema definitions, state transitions, CRUD operations, merge/reorganize, sync engine, and the public API surface.

- Files: 43
- Cohesion: 0.44, Coupling: 0.56
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/add.ts, src/cli/commands/reorganize.ts, src/core/cascade-reset.ts, src/core/delete.ts, src/core/facets.ts (+16 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/prd-domain-operations/context.md`

### PRD Proposal Pipeline (`prd-proposal-pipeline`)

Core domain services for generating, diffing, deduplicating, decomposing, and reconciling PRD item proposals from LLM analysis output.

- Files: 34
- Cohesion: 0.37, Coupling: 0.63
- Entry points: src/analyze/acknowledge.ts, src/analyze/decompose.ts, src/analyze/dedupe.ts, src/analyze/diff.ts, src/analyze/extract.ts (+7 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/prd-proposal-pipeline/context.md`

### PRD Repair Command (`prd-repair-command`)

Self-contained implementation of the `rex fix` command that detects and repairs structural inconsistencies in the PRD tree.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 0.25, Coupling: 0.75
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/fix.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/prd-repair-command/context.md`

### PRD Structural Validation (`prd-structural-validation`)

Implements PRD integrity checks including structural consistency, epic correlation, and interactive fix prompts surfaced through the validate CLI commands.

- Files: 11
- Cohesion: 0.36, Coupling: 0.64
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/validate.ts, src/core/structural.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/prd-structural-validation/context.md`

### Project Configuration (`project-configuration`)

Root-level project scaffolding: package manifest, TypeScript and test configuration, documentation assets, and AI-readable context files generated by sourcevision.

- Files: 8
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/project-configuration/context.md`

### Rex CLI Command Layer (`rex-cli-command-layer`)

Implements the full suite of rex CLI commands and their supporting output, error handling, and parent-node lifecycle utilities.

- Files: 37
- Cohesion: 0.46, Coupling: 0.54
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/token-format.ts, src/cli/commands/usage.ts, src/cli/errors.ts, src/cli/output.ts, src/cli/validate-input.ts (+5 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/rex-cli-command-layer/context.md`

### Rex E2E Test Suite (`rex-e2e-test-suite`)

Contains all end-to-end CLI and adapter tests that exercise rex commands against real file-system fixtures, validating full-stack behavior from CLI invocation to PRD state.

- Files: 13
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/rex-e2e-test-suite/context.md`

### Rex Runtime State (`rex-runtime-state`)

Persistent runtime data directory holding the live PRD, execution log, project configuration, and workflow state written and read by rex commands.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/rex-runtime-state/context.md`

### Schema Storage and Reporting (`schema-storage-reporting`)

Groups schema validation, the file-system PRD store adapter, and the report command — three loosely related infrastructure concerns that share no strong internal dependency.

- Files: 7
- Cohesion: 0.17, Coupling: 0.83
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/report.ts, src/schema/validate.ts, src/store/file-adapter.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/schema-storage-reporting/context.md`

### Task Selection Engine (`task-selection-engine`)

Implements next-task selection logic including multi-criteria scoring, feature-scoped filtering, and requirements-based prioritization for the `rex next` command.

- Files: 7
- Cohesion: 0.17, Coupling: 0.83
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/next.ts, src/core/next-task.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/task-selection-engine/context.md`

### Tree and DAG Primitives (`tree-dag-primitives`)

Houses the foundational tree traversal, DAG cycle detection, and node-move algorithms that underpin all PRD hierarchy operations.

- Files: 7
- Cohesion: 0.06, Coupling: 0.94
- Entry points: src/core/dag.ts, src/core/move.ts, src/core/tree.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/tree-dag-primitives/context.md`

## Key Dependencies

### Most-Imported Files

- `src/core/tree.ts` (49 imports)
- `src/schema/index.ts` (44 imports)
- `src/store/index.ts` (32 imports)
- `src/cli/commands/constants.ts` (29 imports)
- `src/cli/output.ts` (28 imports)
- `src/cli/errors.ts` (26 imports)
- `src/analyze/reason.ts` (23 imports)
- `src/core/canonical.ts` (17 imports)
- `src/core/stats.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/store/project-config.ts` (9 imports)

### External Packages

- `vitest` (used by 118 files)
- `@n-dx/llm-client` (used by 13 files)
- `zod` (used by 7 files)
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (used by 2 files)
- `ora` (used by 1 files)

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- cli-mcp-interface bypasses the analyze pipeline's sequential abstraction by importing from both llm-analysis-engine (26) and prd-proposal-pipeline (5) rather than through a single facade — a narrow pipeline entry-point export would let the CLI drive the full pipeline without depending on its intermediate stages and would shrink cli-mcp-interface's coupling score by ~31 cross-zone imports [warning] (cli-mcp-interface)
- Zone spans two non-adjacent architectural tiers (CLI dispatch + domain persistence) simultaneously — no single cohesion metric can accurately describe a zone whose files have fundamentally different dependency profiles; the zone must be split before health metrics become meaningful [warning] (integration-adapter-layer)
- sync.ts imports directly from ../../store/index.js, bypassing any interface boundary; the CLI command knows the internal shape of the store rather than depending on an abstract SyncPort — this is a missing ports-and-adapters boundary, not just a misplaced file [critical] (integration-adapter-layer)
- llm-analysis-engine imports from cli-mcp-interface (5 imports) — an analysis engine module depending on CLI-layer code is a tier inversion; the shared symbols should be extracted into a foundation or schema module that both zones consume, eliminating the reverse edge without requiring a broad refactor of either zone [critical] (llm-analysis-engine)
- store-contract.test.ts validates the adapter interface defined in src/store/contracts.ts (integration-adapter-layer zone) — the contract definition and its compliance test live in separate zones, meaning a breaking change to the interface can be made without the zone health tooling detecting that the test zone is affected [warning] (notion-integration-client)
- Zone size of 43 files and 21 entry points is inflated by misclassified CLI command files; do not use these raw numbers to justify architectural refactoring until misclassified files are pinned and re-analyzed [info] (prd-domain-operations)
- src/cli/commands/add.ts and src/cli/commands/reorganize.ts are [cli-command] files misclassified into the domain zone; their intra-CLI imports to constants.ts, errors.ts, and output.ts appear as 'core → unit-cli' cross-zone violations, inflating the bidirectional coupling metric and overstating zone size by at least 2 files [warning] (prd-domain-operations)
- prd-proposal-pipeline participates in two concurrent cycles (with llm-analysis-engine: 27+13, and with prd-domain-operations: 24+11) and additionally has 2 reverse imports into cli-mcp-interface — no single cycle can be resolved in isolation; all three cycles in the analyze sub-graph must be broken in a coordinated pass to restore a DAG structure [critical] (prd-proposal-pipeline)
- One integration test (smart-add-orphaned-parent.test.ts) is stranded in the structural-validation zone while four other integration tests in the same directory have no zone assignment — the integration test layer lacks a dedicated zone, making cross-integration-test dependencies invisible to zone health tooling [warning] (prd-structural-validation)
- epic-correlation.ts imports from src/analyze/dedupe.js — a core utility file pulling in an analyze-subsystem dependency creates a hidden transitive coupling path from validation → core → analyze that bypasses zone boundary enforcement [warning] (prd-structural-validation)
- ... and 4 more

### Patterns

- The MCP server files (mcp.ts, mcp-tools.ts) and the reshape/prune batch-maintenance commands share no import edges with each other inside cli-mcp-interface — they are two independent sub-clusters held together only by the zone boundary. Splitting them into a dedicated mcp-server zone and a maintenance-commands zone would immediately raise cohesion in both resulting zones without changing any imports. [warning] (cli-mcp-interface)
- llm-analysis-engine → prd-proposal-pipeline has 27 imports while prd-proposal-pipeline → llm-analysis-engine has 13 — the heavier flow goes from 'engine' into 'pipeline', which is semantically backwards. The engine consumes the pipeline more than the pipeline consumes the engine, suggesting the zone names should be swapped or the boundary redrawn so the pipeline drives the engine. [warning] (llm-analysis-engine)
- The store-contract.test.ts file encodes the shared adapter interface contract but is permanently anchored to the notion-integration-client zone by co-location; future adapters cannot inherit this contract without crossing a zone boundary, creating a scalability constraint on the adapter plugin system. [warning] (notion-integration-client)
- prd-domain-operations has become the de-facto package hub with 150+ combined inbound imports across all zones — this is the single highest architectural risk concentration in the rex package; a formal internal layering (schema → mutations → sync) should be enforced before the zone grows further [warning] (prd-domain-operations)
- The wide entry-point surface (12 exports) of prd-proposal-pipeline means the sequential pipeline contract (propose → decompose → dedupe → reconcile) is unenforced — consumers can invoke any stage in any order. If pipeline stage ordering is a correctness requirement, the public surface should expose only the top-level entry (propose) and keep intermediate stages internal. [warning] (prd-proposal-pipeline)
- prd-repair-command is structurally identical to task-selection-engine: one CLI command file, one core implementation file, and N test files grouped by proximity — this recurring pattern suggests the zone detection algorithm consistently creates micro-zones around test clusters, not domain clusters [info] (prd-repair-command)
- The project-configuration zone conflates two distinct artifact categories: build/config files (package.json, tsconfig.json, vitest.config.ts) and AI-context outputs (llms.txt, context.md). These have different lifecycles and different consumers — config files are read by tooling, AI context files are consumed by LLMs. This distinction is invisible in the current zone grouping. [info] (project-configuration)
- rex-e2e-test-suite is the only zone in the package that achieves both perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling while spanning 13 files — its structure (one file per CLI command under tests/e2e/) is a repeatable pattern that the fragmented unit zones (unit-cli, unit-analyze, unit) could adopt to improve their own cohesion scores. [info] (rex-e2e-test-suite)
- rex-runtime-state files are consumed via disk reads, not module imports — the zone boundary is logically invisible to static analysis and import-graph enforcement tools. Any linting rules based on import graphs will silently miss cross-zone access to these files. [info] (rex-runtime-state)
- e2e/cli-init.test.ts was pulled into this zone because it imports both validate.ts and file-adapter.ts, demonstrating how e2e tests that import from multiple domain zones can contaminate zone assignments — zone detection should apply a blanket pin rule: tests/e2e/** always anchors to the e2e zone regardless of import neighbors [warning] (schema-storage-reporting)
- ... and 1 more

### Relationships

- cli-mcp-interface imports from core (31), unit-analyze (26), and unit-cli (13) simultaneously — it is the widest fan-out zone in the package. Unlike the orchestration-tier entry points (cli.js, ci.js) which are spawn-only, cli-mcp-interface imports directly from all downstream layers, making it both the highest-coupling zone and the one most resistant to incremental refactoring. [critical] (cli-mcp-interface)
- integration-adapter-layer and the store zone (other zones) together fragment adapter infrastructure across two Louvain communities — files sharing the same architectural role (adapter registry, schemas, Notion adapter) are split between zones, meaning no single zone boundary maps cleanly to the integration subsystem and zone health metrics undercount the true coupling surface. [warning] (integration-adapter-layer)
- store/atomic-write.ts being assigned to llm-analysis-engine by community detection creates a covert cross-zone dependency: analysis code can reach store internals without any gateway indirection. If atomic-write is pinned back to the store zone, any analysis file currently importing it directly will create an undeclared cross-zone import that bypasses the gateway pattern. [critical] (llm-analysis-engine)
- The partial extraction of tree-dag-primitives from prd-domain-operations created an inconsistent domain boundary: some primitives (dag.ts, tree.ts, move.ts) are isolated while their consumers remain in the parent zone — the extraction should either complete (merge unit-3 back) or deepen (extract more primitive layers) [warning] (prd-domain-operations)
- prd-proposal-pipeline and llm-analysis-engine are both rooted in src/analyze/ with no subdirectory boundary — the zone split is a logical artifact of community detection, not a physical boundary. This makes the architectural separation fragile: any new file added to src/analyze/ will be assigned to one zone arbitrarily, and the 27+13 cross-imports will continue to grow as the directory grows. [warning] (prd-proposal-pipeline)
- The mutual 1-import dependency between prd-repair-command and rex-cli-command-layer is likely a shared CLI output utility rather than a domain interface — verify that fix.ts is not importing formatting functions from sibling command files, which would indicate missing shared utility extraction [info] (prd-repair-command)
- epic-correlation.ts and structural.ts appear to originate in prd-structural-validation but flow 17 imports upward into prd-domain-operations; a utility that is more heavily consumed by core than by its own zone has inverted dependency direction — moving these files to core would align the import arrow with the architectural hierarchy. [warning] (prd-structural-validation)
- cli-mcp-interface imports 13 symbols from rex-cli-command-layer, creating a lateral dependency between two CLI-tier zones that should both depend downward on domain — the MCP interface is reaching into CLI command internals instead of calling domain functions directly [warning] (rex-cli-command-layer)
- rex-cli-command-layer is simultaneously imported by three zones (prd-domain-operations 13, cli-mcp-interface 13, integration-adapter-layer 2) making it a secondary hub in the CLI tier; any CLI utility extracted here automatically acquires three upstream dependents [warning] (rex-cli-command-layer)
- schema-storage-reporting is the only zone where dissolution eliminates zero genuine cross-file relationships — all three source files have independent homes in existing zones and share no domain purpose; dissolving this zone would reduce overall zone count by one without losing any architectural signal [warning] (schema-storage-reporting)
- ... and 3 more

### Observations

- 12 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports [warning] (cli-mcp-interface)
- High coupling (0.73) — 31 imports target "prd-domain-operations" [warning] (cli-mcp-interface)
- Low cohesion (0.27) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone [warning] (cli-mcp-interface)
- Cohesion of 0.27 is well below the 0.4 threshold — this zone aggregates unrelated concerns (MCP server, reshape logic, pruning, chunked review) that would benefit from separation. [warning] (cli-mcp-interface)
- Coupling of 0.73 is significantly above the 0.6 warning threshold, driven by 31 outbound imports to core and 26 to unit-analyze — this zone is highly dependent and fragile to upstream changes. [warning] (cli-mcp-interface)
- The cross-zone import path core → cli (13 imports) creates a cycle with cli → core (31 imports); core-layer code should never import from the CLI layer — these imports should be inverted or extracted to a shared module. [warning] (cli-mcp-interface)
- Bidirectional coupling: "prd-domain-operations" ↔ "rex-cli-command-layer" (13+39 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Hub: src/schema/index.ts is imported by 11 zones — cross-cutting dependency [info]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 43 files vs smallest with 4 — uneven decomposition [info]
- Fan-in hotspot: src/schema/index.ts receives calls from 24 files — high-impact module, changes may have wide ripple effects [warning]
- ... and 59 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "cli-mcp-interface" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (cli-mcp-interface)
- reshape-reason.ts should be pinned to its correct zone (cli-mcp-interface or a dedicated reshape zone) via a sourcevision zone pin, rather than relying on directory co-location in src/analyze/ which makes its zone assignment invisible to filesystem navigation [warning] (cli-mcp-interface)
- src/cli/commands/constants.ts (9-zone hub) must be relocated to a foundation or schema zone before any cli-mcp-interface split is attempted — splitting cli-mcp-interface while constants.ts remains inside it will propagate the instability to all 9 consumers simultaneously [critical] (cli-mcp-interface)
- Zone "CLI and MCP Interface" (cli-mcp-interface) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.73, cohesion: 0.27, coupling: 0.73) — requires immediate architectural intervention [critical] (cli-mcp-interface)
- src/analyze/ contains files from 3 zones (cli-mcp-interface, llm-analysis-engine, prd-proposal-pipeline) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- Add a zone detection configuration rule that excludes tests/** files as community members in Louvain partitioning — test files should inherit their source file's zone assignment rather than attract source files into proximity-based micro-zones; this single algorithm change would dissolve all three test-adjacency artifact zones simultaneously [warning]
- Add an architecture policy test asserting that no zone has both cohesion < 0.4 AND coupling > 0.6 simultaneously without a corresponding open PRD task — this converts the dual-threshold breach into a CI failure rather than a passive health warning, preventing new zones from silently entering the warning zone [info]
- Add an intra-package zone-boundary test (parallel to domain-isolation.test.js for cross-package imports) that enumerates declared zone memberships and asserts that files in one zone do not import directly from files in a non-adjacent zone — this would catch tier-skip violations like unit-cli → store and analyze → store/atomic-write that are currently invisible to all existing enforcement [warning]
- Create a dedicated 'adapter-ports' zone pinned to src/store/ports/ containing contracts.ts and any future interface definitions — separating the port definition zone from the adapter implementation zone makes it possible for zone health tooling to detect when implementations bypass the interface [warning]
- Create src/core/public.ts as a stable narrow API surface for prd-domain-operations — expose only the types and functions that CLI-tier consumers need, and redirect the 138+ inbound imports to this surface rather than to internal core files; this decouples consumer zones from core internals and enables safe core restructuring [warning]
- ... and 45 more

## File Inventory

| Path | Role | Archetype | Zone |
|------|------|-----------|------|
| `.claude/settings.local.json` | other |  |  |
| `.rex/config.json` | other |  | rex-runtime-state |
| `.rex/execution-log.jsonl` | other |  | rex-runtime-state |
| `.rex/prd.json` | other |  | rex-runtime-state |
| `.rex/workflow.md` | docs |  | rex-runtime-state |
| `README.md` | docs |  | project-configuration |
| `Rex-F.png` | asset |  | project-configuration |
| `Rex.png` | asset |  | project-configuration |
| `context.md` | docs |  | project-configuration |
| `llms.txt` | docs |  | project-configuration |
| `package.json` | config |  | project-configuration |
| `src/analyze/acknowledge.ts` | source | service | prd-proposal-pipeline |
| `src/analyze/analyze-shared.ts` | source | utility | llm-analysis-engine |
| `src/analyze/consolidation-guard.ts` | source | service | llm-analysis-engine |

| `tsconfig.json` | config |  | project-configuration |
| `vitest.config.ts` | config |  | project-configuration |

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
